content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the milestone CLI changes and found three fix-now issues: interrupted start reservations can wedge planning permanently, show/status misreport replans that are in progress or awaiting answers, and pending requirements runs are discarded on any read error instead of only true stale/not-found cases.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: If the CLI is interrupted after writing a __starting__ reservation, milestone planning becomes permanently wedged. reserve_pending_requirements_run rejects any prefixed pending_requirements_run_id at src/cli/milestone.rs:723-728, but that marker is only replaced after draft_milestone returns from the full requirements pipeline at src/cli/milestone.rs:667-688 and src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs:188-217. A killed plan command therefore leaves no recovery path for later milestone plan invocations. severity: high affected_files: ["src/cli/milestone.rs:667-688", "src/cli/milestone.rs:723-735", "src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs:188-217"] remediation_hint: Persist the real run_id before the long-running pipeline starts, or add stale-reservation timeout/ownership recovery instead of treating every __starting__ marker as live forever.
   Rationale: An interrupted planning run can block all future milestone planning until someone manually edits status.json, and the vulnerable window currently lasts for the entire requirements pipeline.
2. finding_summary: Replanning an already ready/completed/failed milestone never updates the exposed status to show that planning is underway or awaiting answers. The reservation path only sets pending_requirements_run_id at src/cli/milestone.rs:714-735, handle_plan returns early on awaiting_answers at src/cli/milestone.rs:154-161, and both summary/detail views serialize snapshot.status directly while omitting the pending run at src/cli/milestone.rs:61-89 and src/cli/milestone.rs:355-395. As a result, milestone show/status can continue reporting 'ready' or 'completed' even while a replan is blocked on clarification. severity: medium affected_files: ["src/cli/milestone.rs:61-89", "src/cli/milestone.rs:154-161", "src/cli/milestone.rs:355-395", "src/cli/milestone.rs:714-735"] remediation_hint: Either derive an effective display status from pending_requirements_run_id and the referenced requirements run, or transition the milestone snapshot back to planning while a replan is outstanding and expose the pending run in the JSON/detail output.
   Rationale: These commands are supposed to reflect live milestone state; hiding an active replan or awaiting-answers state misleads operators and downstream tooling.
3. finding_summary: load_pending_requirements_run silently clears the pending pointer for every read_run failure at src/cli/milestone.rs:615-620, even though the filesystem adapter distinguishes missing runs from corrupt or otherwise unreadable run.json files at src/adapters/fs.rs:5427-5440. A malformed or transiently unreadable requirements/<run>/run.json will therefore be treated as stale state and replaced by a brand-new planning run, orphaning the original clarification/bundle data instead of surfacing the underlying corruption. severity: medium affected_files: ["src/cli/milestone.rs:615-620", "src/adapters/fs.rs:5427-5440"] remediation_hint: Only clear pending_requirements_run_id for a definite not-found/stale case; propagate CorruptRecord and other I/O failures so operators can repair the existing requirements run rather than forking a new one.
   Rationale: This converts recoverable data corruption or transient I/O problems into silent state loss and duplicate planning runs, which is a correctness issue for milestone workflows.
